title: Cercodia prostrata

This family is part of the order Lamiales, which also includes other families of flowering plants such as Acanthaceae and Lamiaceae. The order Lamiales is a part of the clade Asterids, which is one of the two main groups of flowering plants, the other being the clade Rosids. Further classification of Cercodia prostrata may involve its genus or species-level taxonomy, but its basic classification within the Gesneriaceae family is well established.
uses: Cercodia prostrata, also known as Coralroot, has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits.
